hadiths.ar:
563  -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
ق ِى  إَّلْمُّصِي ن َّه ِ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} was asked about the meaning of the 
verse from Allah’s {SWT} Book: "And there is no town but We shall destroy it before the 
Day of Resurrection or punish it with severe punishment."  
(Surah Al-Isra 17:58) 
Imam {a.s} said: "It refers to perishing through death”. 
[REFERENCES] Tafsir al-Ayashi, vol. 2, p. 297 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 186 • Tafsir Al-Burhan, 
vol. 3, p. 541 • Tafsir Al -Burhan, vol. 3, p. 541 •  Tafsir Nur Al -Thaqalayn, Part 3, p. 178 • Tafseer Kanz Al -
Daqaiq, Part 7, p. 433 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 562
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٦٢

hadiths.ar:
564   -  وَّ سُّن ِلَّ إ َّن ُّو إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ مُّوسَّى ن ْن ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّق ُّولُّ لأِن ْن ِهِ إ َّوْ لأِن ْن َّي ِهِ ن ِا َّن ِى  إ َّن ْث َّ وَّ إ ُّمِّى  إ َّوْ 
ن ِا َّن َّوَّى  َّ إ َّن ْث َّ إ َّ ي َّزَّى ن ِذ َّلِك َّ ن َّا ْساً ق َّق َّال ذْ مَّاي َّا ق َّلاَّ ن َّا ْسَّ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"It is not for you to offer us condolences, but it is for us to offer you condolences. 
Indeed, it is for you to congratulate us, because you share in our misfortune”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 187 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 551 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 563
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٦٣

hadiths.en:
Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim {a.s} was asked about a man who 
says to his son or daughter, "May my father and mother (or my parents) be ransomed 
for you."  
The Imam responded: "If his parents are alive, I consider this to be an act of 
disobedience (toward them). If they have passed away, then there is no problem."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 564
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٦٤

hadiths.ar:
566   -  وَّق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم وي َّعَّالَّى  ي َّطَّو َّلَّ عَّلَّى عِن َّاذِةِ ن ِي َّلاَّب ٍ إ َّلْق َّى عَّلَّي ْهِمُّ إَّلزِّي حَّ ن َّعْذَّ إَّلز ُّوحِ وَّ لَّوْ لأَّ  
ذ َّلِك َّ مَّا ذَّق َّن َّ جَّمِي م  جَّمِي ماً وَّ إ َّلْق َّى عَّلَّي ْهِمُّ إَّلس َّلْوَّة َّ ن َّعْذَّ إَّلْمُّصِي ن َّه ِ وَّ لَّوْ لأَّ ذ َّلِك َّ لأَّن ْق َّطَّغَّ إَّل ن  َّسْلُّ وَّ إ َّلْق َّى عَّلَّى هَّذ ِةِ إَّلْحَّن  َّه ِ 
إَّلذ َّإن  َّه َّ وَّ لَّوْ لأَّ ذ َّلِك َّ لَّكَّن َّز َّهَّا مُّلُّوكُّهُّمْ كَّمَّا ي َّكْن ِز ُّون َّ إَّلذ  َّهَّث َّ وَّ  إَّلْق ِص  َّه َّ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Patience is of two types: patience during a calamity, which is good and beautiful, and 
greater than this is patience in refraining from what Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, 
has forbidden, as it acts as a barrier for you." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 565
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٦٥

hadiths.ar:
567 - وَّق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم ن َّا ن ِق َّص َّان ِهِ وَّ سَّل َّمْن َّا  
لِأ َّمْزِةِ وَّ لَّي ْسَّ لَّن َّا إ َّن ْ ن َّكْزَّةَّ مَّا إ َّجَّث  َّ إَّلل َّهُّ لَّن َّا .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Indeed, Allah {SWT}, Blessed and Exalted, has favored His servants with three things:  
He has sent them wind after a calm, for without it, no one would bury a loved one;  
He has granted them solace after a calamity, for without it, lineage would be cut off;  
and He has put sustenance in this grain, for without it, kings would hoard it as they 
hoard gold and silver." 
[REFERENCES] Al Mahasin, Part 2, p. 316 • Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 227 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 
187 • Ilal al-Sharai’ Vol. 1, p. 299 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 559 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 277 • Bihar Al Anwar, 
Vol. 78, p. 247 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 100, p. 87 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 566
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٦٦

hadiths.ar: 568 - وَّق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم .
hadiths.en:
(Muhammad ibn Ali ibn Al-Hussain narrates from) Imam Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"We, the people of the household (AhlulBayt {a.s}), may become distressed before a 
calamity occurs, but when the command of Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, comes 
to pass, we are content with His decree and submit to His command. We do not have 
the right to dislike what Allah {SWT} has chosen for us."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 187 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 276 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 567
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٦٧

hadiths.ar:
569   -  وَّق َّال إِن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  لَّي ْلَّى لِلص َّاذِق ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  إ َّى  ُّ س َّى ْءٍ إ َّجْلَّى مِم َّا ج َّلَّق َّ إَّلل َّهُّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ق َّق َّال 
ق َّق َّالَّ إ َّى  ُّ س َّى ْءٍ إ َّمَّز ُّ مِم َّا ج َّلَّق َّ إَّلل َّهُّ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ق َّال  عَّلَّى ج َّلْق ِهِ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Al-Sadiq {a.s} said: "Whoever fears being overwhelmed by grief due to a 
calamity should let their tears flow, for it will bring them relief."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 187 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 564 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, 
p. 280 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 79, p. 105 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 568
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٦٨

hadiths.ar:
570  - وَّق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم كُّلِّ س َّعْزَّة ٍ 
ن ُّورإً ي َّوْمَّ إَّلْق ِي َّامَّه ِ .
hadiths.en:
Ibn Abi Layla said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s}:  
"What is the sweetest of what Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, has created?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "A young child."  
Ibn Abi Layla then asked: "What is the most bitter of what Allah {SWT} has created?"  
The Imam answered: "Losing him."  
Ibn Abi Layla then said: "I testify that you are the proofs of Allah {SWT} for His creation." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 188 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 548 • Ithbāt al-Hudāt, Part 4, 
p. 148 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 569
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٦٩

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"There is no servant who passes their hand over the head of an orphan out of 
compassion for them except that Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, grants them a 
light for every strand of hair (they touch) on the Day of Judgment."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 188 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 557 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, 
p. 286 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 21, p. 374 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 570
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٧٠

hadiths.ar:
572  -  وَّق َّال رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ :  «
ي َّلِي ن ُّ ق َّلْن ُّهُّ ن ِا ِذ ْن ِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز  َّ وَّ ج َّل َّ ق َّا ِن  َّ لِلْي َّي ِي مِ جَّق  اً . وَّ رُّوِى َّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
It is reported: "Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, writes for the person, for 
every strand of hair that their hand passes over (on the head of the orphan), a good 
deed." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 188 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 286 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 571
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٧١

hadiths.ar:
573  -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّ :   «وي َّعَّالَّىمَّن ْ هَّذ َّإ إَّل َّذ ِى  إ َّن ْكَّى عَّن ْذِى َّ  
إَّل َّذ ِى  سَّلَّن ْي ُّهُّ إ َّن َّوَّي ْهِ ق ِى  صِع َّزِةِ ق َّوَّ عِز  َّي ِى  وَّ ج َّلاَّلِى  وَّ إِرْي ِق َّاعِى  ق ِى  مَّكَّان ِى  لأَّ ي ُّسْكِي ُّهُّ عَّن ْذ  مُّو ْ مِن   إ ِلأ َّ إ َّوْج َّن ْث ُّ لَّهُّ إَّلْح َّن  َّه َّ .
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} said:  
"Whoever finds hardness in their heart should bring an orphan close, show kindness 
to them, and pass their hand over their head; this will soften their heart, by the 
permission of Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic. Indeed, the orphan has a right."  
It is also reported that He said: "He should seat the orphan at his table and pass his 
hand over their head, and this will soften his heart."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 188 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 557 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, 
p. 286 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 21, p. 375 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 188 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, 
p. 287 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 572
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٧٢

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"When an orphan cries, the Throne of Allah {SWT} shakes, and Allah {SWT}, Blessed and 
Exalted, says,  
'Who is it that has made My servant cry, the one from whom I took his parents in his 
childhood? By My might, majesty, and elevated status, no believer will calm him 
except that I will make Paradise obligatory for him.'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 188 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 558 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, 
p. 287 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 21, p. 446 • Kulliyat Hadith Qudsi, Vol. 1, p. 674 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 573
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٧٣

hadiths.ar:
575  -  وَّق َّال رَّسُّولُّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ :  «وي َّعَّالَّىكَّزِةَّ لِى  سِث  َّ ج ِصَّالٍ وَّ  كَّزِهْي ُّهُّن  َّ لِلْا َّوْصِي َّاءِ 
مِن ْ وُّلْذِى  وَّ إ َّي ْن َّاعِهِمْ مِن ْ ن َّعْذِى  إَّلْعَّن َّث َّ ق ِى  إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ وَّ إَّلز َّق َّث َّ ق ِى  إَّلص َّوْمِ وَّ إَّلْمَّن  َّ ن َّعْذَّ إَّلص َّ ذَّق َّه ِ وَّ إ ِي ْي َّان َّ إَّلْمَّسَّاج ِذِ ج ُّن ُّن اً 
وَّ إَّلي  َّطَّل ُّغَّ ق ِى  إَّلذ ُّورِ وَّ إَّلص  َّحِك َّ ن َّي ْن َّ إَّلْق ُّن ُّ ورِ .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Whoever offers children (to Allah {SWT}, i.e., loses them and bears the loss patiently, 
seeking reward from Allah {SWT}), they will shield him from the Fire, by the permission of 
Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 219 • Al -Kafi, Volume 3, p. 220 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 
188 • Thawab al -A’mal wa Iqab al -A’mal, Part 1, p. 196 • Al -Amali (by Al -Saduq) Vol. 1, p. 541 • Jami’ al -
Akhbar, Part 1, p. 105 • Muskkin al -Fu’ād, Part 1, p. 20 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 547 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 548 • 
Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 244 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 574
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٧٤

hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} said:  
"Indeed, Allah {SWT}, Blessed and Exalted, dislikes six traits for me, and I also dislike 
them for the successors (Imams) from my descendants and for their followers after 
me: playing around during prayer, inappropriate speech during fasting, boasting after 
giving charity, entering mosques while in a state of major ritual impurity, peeking into 
people’s houses, and laughing among graves." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 188 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 2, p. 71 • Al -
Khisal, Part 1, p. 327 • The Virtues of the Three Months, Part 1, p. 76 • Al -Amali (by Al-Saduq) Vol. 1, p. 62 • 
Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 232 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 233 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 9, p. 452 • Bihar Al-Anwar, 
Vol. 76, p. 277 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 78, p. 61 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 575
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٧٥

hadiths.ar:
577  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ: إ َّن  َّ إَّلسِّن ْذِى  َّ ن ْن َّ س َّاهَّك َّ ق َّالَّ لِأ َّن ِى إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ مُّوسَّى ن ْن ِ ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ ُّجِث  ُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّذَّعَّن ِى  عَّلَّى إ َّن ْ 
إ ُّكَّق ِّن َّك َّ ق َّق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Whatever is placed on the grave that is not from the soil of the grave itself is a burden 
upon the deceased." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 189 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 521 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, 
p. 202 • Al-Fusul al-Muhimmah, Part 2, p. 42 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 576
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٧٦

hadiths.ar:
578   -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ  إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :   «
ق ِي كُّمْ ي َّا مَّعْس َّزَّ إَّلس ِّي عَّه ِ .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated that Al -Sindi ibn Shahak said to Abu Al -Hasan Imam Musa ibn 
Jafar Al-Kadhim {a.s} ; "I would like you to allow me to provide your shroud."  
Imam {a.s} replied: "We, the people of the household (AhlulBayt {a.s}), have our 
pilgrimage, dowries of our women, and shrouds taken from the purity of our own 
wealth."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 189 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 355 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, 
p. 55 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 577
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٧٧

hadiths.ar:
579 - وَّق َّال إ َّمِي زُّ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : «. 
وإج ي لف  مس ان ح ن ا ق ى  معن ى هذ إ إلح ن ز ق ق ال محمذ ن ن  إلحسن  إلصق ار   ر:  هو ج ذذ ن الح ي م لأ ع ي ز، وكان  س ي ح ن ا محمذ ن ن   
إلحسن  ن ن  إ جمذ ن ن  إلولي ذ رص ى  إلله عن ه ي حكى  عن ه   إ ن ه ق ال: لأ  ي ح ور  ي ح ذي ذ إلق ن ز ولأ ي طي ي ن  ج مي عه ن عذ مزور إلأ ي ام علي ه  
و ن عذ ما طي ن  ق ى  إلأ ول ولكن  إ ذ إ ماب  مي ث  وطي ن  ق ن زة ق ح ان ز  إ ن  ي زم سان ز إلق ن ور من  ع ي ز إ ن  ي ح ذذ.  وذ كز عن  سعذ ن ن   
عن ذ إلله  رجمه إللها ن ه كان  ي ق ول: إ ن ما هو من  جذذ ق ن زإ ن الحاء ع ي ز إلمعح مه  ي عن ى  ن ه من  سن م ق ن زإ.  وذ كز   عن  إ جمذ ن ن  إ ن ى  
عن ذإ لله إلن زق ى  إ ن ه ق ال: إ ن ما هو من  ج ذب  ق ن زإ، و ي ق سي ز إلح ذب    إلق ن ز ق لا ن ذ رى  ما عن ى ن ه، وإلذ ى  إ ذ هث  إ لي ه إ ن ه ج ذذ 
ن الح ي م ومعن اة ن ن س  ق ن زإ لأن  من  ن ن س  ق ن زإ ق ق ذ ج ذذة وإ جوح  إ لى ي ح ذي ذة وق ذ ج عله ج ذي ا محق ورإ.  وإ ق ول: إ ن  إلي ح ذي ذ 
على إلمعن ى إلذ ى  ذ هث  إ لي ه  محمذ ن ن  إلحسن  إلصق ار، و إلي حذي ذ ن الحاء ع ي ز إلمعح مه  إلذ ى  ذ هث  إ لي ه سعذ ن ن  عن ذ إلله، 
وإلذ ى  ق اله إلن زق ى  من  إ ن ه ج ذب  كله ذإج ل ق ى  معن ى إلحذي ث  ، وإ ن  من  ج الف  إلأ مام علي ه إلسلام ق ى  إلي ح ذي ذ وإلي سن ي م  
وإلن ن س  وإسي حل س ي ن ا من  ذ لك  ق ق ذ ج زح  من  إلأسلام . وإلذ ى  إ ق وله ق ى  ق وله علي ه إلسلام: من  مي ل مي الأ ي عن ى  ن ه إ ن ه من   
إ ن ذع ن ذ وذعا إ لي ها، إ و وص غ ذي ن ا ق ق ذ ج زح  من  إلأسلام، وق ولى  ق ى  ذ لك  ق ول إ ن مي ى  علي هم إلسلام، ق ا ن  إ صن ث  ق من  إلله على  
إ لسن ي هم وإ ن  إ ج طا ب  ق من  عن ذ ن ق سى .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Our enemies die of plague, while you (our Shi’ite) die of ailments of the stomach. 
Verily, this is a sign among you, O’ group of Shi'a." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 189 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 24, p. 201 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 578
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٧٨

hadiths.ar:
580  -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ عَّم َّارٍ إَّلس َّان َّاطِى ِّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ: سُّن ِلَّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّن ِ إَّلْمَّي ِّث ِ هَّلْ  ي َّن ْلَّى ج َّسَّذُّةُّ ق َّق َّال 
جَّي  َّى لأَّ ي َّن ْق َّى لَّحْم  وَّ لأَّ  عَّط ْم  إ ِلأ َّ طِي ن َّي ُّهُّ إَّل َّي ِى  ج ُّلِق َّ مِن ْهَّا ق َّا ِن  َّهَّا لأَّ ي َّن ْلَّى ي َّن ْق َّى ق ِى  إَّلْق َّن ْزِ مُّسْي َّذِي زَّة ً جَّي  َّى ي ُّح ْلَّق َّ مِ ن ْهَّا 
كَّمَّا ج ُّلِق َّ إ َّو َّلَّ مَّز َّة ٍ .
hadiths.en:
The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said:  
"Whoever renovates a grave or creates a figure (idol/statue) has exited Islam."  
[AL SADUQ] 
There is a difference of opinion among our scholars regarding the meaning of this 
report. Muhammad ibn al -Hasan al-Saffar, may Allah {SWT} have mercy on him, stated 
that the correct reading is jaddada only, meaning "renovated."  
Our elder Muhammad ibn al-Hasan ibn Ahmad ibn al-Walid, reported that He said : "It 
is not permissible to renovate a grave or apply clay over the entire grave after a period 
of time has passed, once it was initially covered. However, if a person dies and h is 
grave is covered with clay, then it is permissible to repair other graves without 
renovating them."  
It was also narrated that Sa'd ibn Abdullah, may Allah {SWT} have mercy on him, said that 
the term should be read as haddada (with an "ha"), meaning "to raise" or "mark" a 
grave. 
Ahmad ibn Abi Abdullah al -Barqi mentioned : that the term is jaddatha (referring to 
"grave" or "burial"). However, the exact meaning was unclear.  
I prefer the interpretation of jaddada with a "j," meaning "to renovate a grave," as 
renovation would involve reopening or modifying the grave, thereby making it a 
disturbed site in need of renewal. 
In addition, it is explained that the term "creating a figure" means introducing 
innovations or calling to them, or establishing an unlawful practice (e.g., creating or 
promoting a religion), which would lead one out of Islam. My interpretation aligns with 
that of my Imams {a.s}; if it is correct, it is due to Allah’s {SWT} guidance through their 
words, and if it is mistaken, it is from my own self. 
[REFERENCES] Al Mahasin, Part 2, p. 612 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 189 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Part 1, p. 459 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 534 • Wasail Al-Shia, Part 3, p. 208 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 5, p. 306 • Al -
Fusul al-Muhimmah, Part 2, p. 43 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 76, p. 285 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 79, p. 16 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 579
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٧٩

hadiths.ar:
581  -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
ي َّطْعَّمَّ مِن ْهَّا س َّي ْن اً .
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Ammar al-Sabati that He said: Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad 
Al-Sadiq {a.s} was asked whether the body of the deceased decays.  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, until there remains no flesh or bones except for the original clay 
from which he was created. It does not decay but remains in the grave, encircling 
itself, until he is recreated from it just as he was created the first time."  
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 251 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 191 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 687 
• Al-Fusul al-Muhimmah, Part 1, p. 341 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 7, p. 43 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 57, p. 357 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 580
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٢٧، ح ٥٨٠

hadiths.ar:
582  -  وَّق َّال إَّلن  َّن ِى  ُّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ :  «
ق َّق َّالَّ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ ۝ وَّ مٰا كٰان َّ إَّللّٰهُّ لِي ُّعَّذ ِّن َّهُّمْ وَّ إ َّن ْث َّ ق ِي هِمْ  ۝»   
وَّ إ َّم َّا مُّق َّارَّق َّي ِى  إ ِي  َّاكُّمْ ق َّا ِن  َّ إ َّعْمَّالَّكُّمْ ي ُّعْزَّض ُّ عَّلَّى  َّ كُّل َّ ي َّوْمٍ ق َّمَّا كَّان َّ مِن ْ جَّسَّن ٍ إِسْي َّز َّذْب ُّ إ لل َّهَّ لَّكُّمْ وَّ مَّا كَّان َّ مِن ْ ق َّن ِي حٍ 
إِسْي َّع ْق َّزْب ُّ إَّلل َّهَّ لَّكُّمْ ب َّ رَّمِي ماً ق َّق َّال وي َّعَّالَّى  جَّز َّمَّ 
لُّحُّومَّن َّا عَّلَّى إَّلْأ َّرْض ِ إ َّن ْ ي َّطْعَّمَّ مِن ْهَّا س َّي ْن اً .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Indeed, Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, has forbidden our bones from being 
consumed by the earth and has forbidden worms from eating our flesh."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 191 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 25, p. 690 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 27, H. 581
